what_it_does: A generated, self-contained browser runtime (dc-runtime) that loads React 18 from unpkg, parses custom <x-dc> design-component documents, compiles their HTML into React element builders, evaluates optional per-component logic classes, manages a live component registry, supports streaming placeholders, loads external JSX/JS modules, injects head elements, and exposes a window API for editor/host integration.
observations: The file is a generated IIFE bundle produced from dc-runtime/src/*.ts and marked do-not-edit.
It bootstraps React and ReactDOM from unpkg with hard-coded SRI hashes if they are not already on window.
It parses an <x-dc> block plus a script[data-dc-script] with JSON data-props and preview metadata.
Template compilation encodes camelCase attributes, raw table tags, import tags, and helmet directives before walking the DOM.
A hand-rolled expression resolver handles literals, paths, bracket access, equality, negation, and parenthesized expressions.
Components are rendered through a React class component (StreamableComponent) that hosts an author-provided logic class extending StreamableLogic.
The registry tracks html, compiled template, Logic class, streaming flags, version, and subscriber callbacks per component name.
Unresolved values render animated skeleton placeholders when html or js streaming is active.
External modules are fetched, optionally transformed with Babel standalone, and resolved from module.exports or captured window globals.
Helmet-style <sc-helmet> content is injected into document.head, with streaming-aware partial skipping.
Circular imports are detected via an AncestorContext React context.
The runtime exposes window methods such as __dcUpdate, __dcSetProps, __dcBoot, __dcAnnotatedTemplate, __dcTemplateSource, and __dcRegistry.
User logic and external modules are executed with the Function constructor, annotated with nosemgrep suppressions.
standouts: It is both a template-to-React compiler and a live, hot-updatable component registry in one file.
Streaming placeholders are first-class: unresolved template values become animated skeletons rather than empty nodes.
It loads and transpiles external JSX/JS modules on the fly using Babel standalone from a CDN.
It uses new Function to evaluate author-provided component logic and fetched module code.
conventions: Generated Babel helpers (__defProp, __publicField) prefix the bundle.
React is always accessed through window.React / window.ReactDOM getter functions.
Attribute encoding normalizes camelCase via sc-camel-* prefixes and raw table tags via sc-raw-* aliases.
Errors are logged to the console and rendered as inline placeholder/error UI instead of crashing the page.
data-dc-tpl IDs are stamped on source nodes so rendered nodes can be mapped back to template nodes.
concerns: evalDcLogic and external module execution rely on new Function, which is risky if content is untrusted.
React and Babel are loaded from hard-coded unpkg URLs with no documented fallback or offline path.
The equality parser is hand-rolled and may mishandle complex expressions or operator precedence.
resolvePath silently returns undefined for malformed paths without surfacing which segment failed.
StreamableComponent calls componentWillUnmount on a logic instance during hot-swap even if it never mounted.
The external loader mutates window and polls globals for up to 30 seconds.
Helmet injects script/link elements into document.head without visible sanitization of src/href values.
A generated runtime artifact is checked into a templates directory, which is unusual for a build output.
notes: This is the generated browser runtime that makes .dc.html design components renderable as React apps and bridges them to an external editor or host.
questions: What host or build step provides the initial <x-dc> markup and props for the capital-raise templates?
Are the unpkg URLs and SRI hashes acceptable for all deployment environments, including offline or CSP-locked ones?
How is user-authored logic code sandboxed given that it runs through the Function constructor?
What signals streaming mode start and completion from the host/editor?
Is there a separate build process that regenerates this file, and why is the generated artifact committed under templates?
purpose_vs_actual: The header says it is generated from dc-runtime TypeScript sources and should not be edited; the content matches that claim. Its placement in a templates folder is slightly unexpected for a runtime build artifact.
apparent_themes: Design-component documents as live, renderable artifacts.
Streaming-aware incremental rendering with skeleton placeholders.
Editor/host integration through a window-level bridge API.
Self-contained runtime with minimal external dependencies beyond a React CDN.
Resilience through error boundaries, placeholders, and graceful degradation.
intention_signals: The file tries to turn static .dc.html templates into interactive React components while keeping the authoring surface simple (HTML plus an optional logic class), and to let an editor stream partial updates to a running preview.
novelty: It combines a lightweight template expression language, React class-component hosting, live logic hot-swap, on-demand JSX/JS module loading via Babel standalone, and streaming skeleton UI in a single browser runtime.
contribution: It contributes the browser-side execution engine for design components: parsing and compiling templates, evaluating logic classes, maintaining a live registry with subscriptions, resolving external modules, injecting head/pseudo styles, and exposing an editor integration API.
summary_for_embedding: A generated, self-bootstrapping browser runtime that compiles custom <x-dc> HTML templates into React components, evaluates per-component logic, manages a live registry, loads external JSX/JS modules, and exposes an editor integration API.
intention_for_embedding: To enable design-component documents to render and update interactively inside a browser preview or host application.

symbols.does: Helper to define or assign an object property.
symbols.description: Babel-style runtime helper used by __publicField; it defines a property with a descriptor when the key already exists, otherwise assigns directly.

symbols.does: Helper to declare a public class field.
symbols.description: Babel-style runtime helper that normalizes symbol keys to strings and uses __defNormalProp to set field values on class instances.

symbols.does: Returns the global React object.
symbols.description: Looks up window.React and throws if React is not loaded yet; used throughout the runtime to create elements and access React APIs.

symbols.does: Returns the global ReactDOM object.
symbols.description: Looks up window.ReactDOM and throws if ReactDOM is not loaded yet; used to mount roots or render legacy-style.

symbols.does: Shorthand for React.createElement.
symbols.description: A thin wrapper that calls getReact().createElement with the supplied arguments, serving as the runtime's JSX replacement.

symbols.does: Parses an <x-dc> template from a DOM document.
symbols.description: Extracts the innerHTML of the first <x-dc> element, the text of script[data-dc-script], and the JSON data-props/preview object.

symbols.does: Parses an <x-dc> template from a raw HTML string.
symbols.description: Uses DOMParser to find the <x-dc> block and script[data-dc-script], returning template, JS, props, and preview.

symbols.does: Parses the JSON data-props attribute.
symbols.description: Extracts the $preview object and all non-$ prefixed keys as props, returning null when the attribute is missing or invalid JSON.

symbols.does: Derives a component name from a URL path.
symbols.description: Decodes the final path segment and strips .dc.html or .html extensions, defaulting to Root.

symbols.does: Chooses the root component name for the current document.
symbols.description: Uses the location pathname if it ends in .dc.html, otherwise falls back to the document baseURI pathname, then applies dcNameFromPath.

symbols.does: Decodes a URI string safely.
symbols.description: Wraps decodeURIComponent in a try/catch and returns the original string on failure.

symbols.does: Bootstraps the runtime on the current document.
symbols.description: Parses the document's <x-dc> block, registers it in the runtime, replaces <x-dc> with a #dc-root host, injects full-page CSS when not in preview mode, and renders the root component via ReactDOM.

symbols.does: React wrapper that re-renders on registry updates.
symbols.description: Subscribes to the root registry entry and renders the root DC component with any prop overrides.

symbols.does: Subscriber callback that increments tick state.
symbols.description: Used inside StandaloneRoot to force a re-render when the registry entry notifies subscribers.

symbols.does: Evaluates a template expression against a value map.
symbols.description: Handles parenthesized expressions, equality operators, negation, literals, numbers, quoted strings, and path lookups; returns undefined for empty or unresolvable expressions.

symbols.does: Checks whether parentheses wrap the whole expression.
symbols.description: Scans character depth to confirm the outer pair encloses the entire expression, used by resolve to unwrap redundant parentheses.

symbols.does: Finds a top-level equality or inequality operator.
symbols.description: Scans the expression outside brackets/parentheses for ==, ===, !=, or !== and returns the operator and its index.

symbols.does: Resolves a dotted/bracketed path against a value map.
symbols.description: Starts with an identifier, then walks .property and [expression] segments, returning undefined when any segment is missing or malformed.

symbols.does: Encodes template HTML for the compiler.
symbols.description: Expands self-closing import tags, renames helmet to sc-helmet, converts camelCase attributes to sc-camel-* kebab form, and swaps raw table tags for alias tags.

symbols.does: Converts kebab-case strings to camelCase.
symbols.description: Used to restore original camelCase attribute names after the compiler encodes them as sc-camel-* attributes.

symbols.does: Converts a CSS declaration string to an object.
symbols.description: Splits on semicolons, maps each property:value pair, preserving CSS custom property names and camelCasing the rest.

symbols.does: Compiles an attribute value into a getter function.
symbols.description: Returns a function that either resolves a whole {{...}} expression, interpolates multiple {{...}} holes, or returns the literal string unchanged.

symbols.does: Collects React props and pseudo-classes from a DOM element.
symbols.description: Iterates attributes, skipping sc-name and data-dc-tpl, normalizes event and special attribute names, compiles values, and records style-* pseudo classes and hint-size.

symbols.does: Extracts host-positioning styles from a style value.
symbols.description: Parses a style string or object and keeps only layout-related properties such as position, inset, width, height, z-index, and transform for the host wrapper.

symbols.does: Compiles template HTML into a render function.
symbols.description: Encodes the HTML, stamps data-dc-tpl IDs, walks the DOM into builder functions, and returns a function that renders React elements for given values and context.

symbols.does: The render function produced by compileTemplate.
symbols.description: Maps the compiled child builders over the provided value map and render context, returning an array of React nodes.

symbols.does: Compiles all child nodes of a DOM node.
symbols.description: Maps each child through walk and filters out null builders.

symbols.does: Dispatches a DOM node to the appropriate compiler.
symbols.description: Routes text nodes, sc-for, sc-if, x-import, sc-helmet, dc-import, and plain elements to their specialized walkers.

symbols.does: Logs a warning once per unresolved template hole.
symbols.description: Deduplicates warnings by component name and message, then logs to console when a {{...}} expression cannot be resolved outside streaming mode.

symbols.does: Compiles a text node with interpolation holes.
symbols.description: Splits text on {{...}} and returns a builder that renders literal segments, resolved values, React elements/arrays, or placeholder spans for missing values.

symbols.does: Compiles a <sc-for> loop directive.
symbols.description: Reads the list expression and as name, compiles child builders, and renders them once per array item with item and $index in scope; falls back to hint placeholders during streaming.

symbols.does: Compiles a <sc-if> conditional directive.
symbols.description: Evaluates the value expression and renders children only when truthy, using a hint value during streaming.

symbols.does: Compiles a <dc-import> component reference.
symbols.description: Reads the component name, compiles props and children, and returns a builder that creates the named DC component via the host component resolver.

symbols.does: Compiles an <x-import> external module reference.
symbols.description: Resolves the module URL and export/global name, loads the external module, and renders the imported component or a placeholder/error node.

symbols.does: Evaluates a bindable name attribute.
symbols.description: Compiles the attribute getter result to a string and rejects values still containing {{, used by walkXImport for component and global names.

symbols.does: Compiles a plain HTML element.
symbols.description: Collects props, unwraps raw tag aliases, compiles children, and returns a builder that creates the real DOM element with React props.

symbols.does: Evaluates the component logic source code.
symbols.description: Uses the Function constructor to execute the script text in a sandbox with DCLogic, StreamableLogic, and React, returning the exported Component class.

symbols.does: Compares two objects shallowly ignoring children.
symbols.description: Used by StreamableComponent to decide whether a previously failed logic constructor can be retried.

symbols.does: React component for a loading/error placeholder.
symbols.description: Renders a sized div with optional error text, used while templates or external modules are unresolved or failed.

symbols.does: Converts a hint-size string to min dimensions.
symbols.description: Parses the width,height hint and returns a style object with minWidth and minHeight for streaming hosts.

symbols.does: Creates the DC component class and dispatcher registry.
symbols.description: Builds StreamableComponent, a React class that hosts logic classes, handles errors, hot-swaps logic, and provides getDC dispatcher functions.

symbols.does: React class that renders a design component instance.
symbols.description: Manages logic lifecycle, registry subscriptions, error boundaries, streaming flags, circular-import detection, and renders the compiled template with merged props and logic values.

symbols.does: Returns or creates a dispatcher for a named DC.
symbols.description: Looks up a cached dispatcher or creates a functional React component that subscribes to registry updates and renders StreamableComponent.

symbols.does: Functional React wrapper for a named DC.
symbols.description: Subscribes to registry updates and renders the StreamableComponent for the given name.

symbols.does: Subscriber callback inside Dispatcher.
symbols.description: Increments local tick state to force re-render when the registry entry changes.

symbols.does: Checks whether a name looks like a custom element.
symbols.description: Returns true if the name contains a hyphen and no dot, used to decide global polling strategy.

symbols.does: Checks whether a value is a renderable React type.
symbols.description: Accepts functions that are not element classes, or objects with a $$typeof symbol.

symbols.does: Walks a dotted path on an object.
symbols.description: Splits the name on dots and returns undefined if any segment is missing.

symbols.does: Factory for the external module loader.
symbols.description: Maintains a cache of fetched JS/JSX modules, loads Babel for JSX transformation, resolves named exports and window globals, and reports missing components.

symbols.does: Loads Babel standalone from unpkg.
symbols.description: Injects a script tag for @babel/standalone if not already present, returning a promise.

symbols.does: Fetches and evaluates an external module.
symbols.description: Fetches the URL, optionally transforms JSX with Babel, runs the code via Function with React/module/exports, captures new window globals, and caches the result.

symbols.does: Resolves a named export from a cached module.
symbols.description: Looks up the named export or default from module.exports or captured globals, logging once if missing.

symbols.does: Polls the global scope for a component name.
symbols.description: Checks window or customElements repeatedly for up to 30 seconds, then bumps the registry when found.

symbols.does: Single polling iteration for waitForGlobal.
symbols.description: Tests for the global/custom element and schedules the next check or notifies resolution.

symbols.does: Resolves a global-scope component reference.
symbols.description: Handles custom elements, dotted window paths, and globals captured from loaded modules, falling back to the name string for unknown custom elements.

symbols.does: Returns a load or resolution error for a module.
symbols.description: Looks up cached module errors or missing-export messages for diagnostics.

symbols.does: Checks whether a function is an HTMLElement subclass.
symbols.description: Used to distinguish web component classes from React components when resolving globals.

symbols.does: Factory for head-element injection.
symbols.description: Compiles <sc-helmet> children into a function that injects script/link/meta/title into document.head, deduplicating by key and handling streaming partials.

symbols.does: Compiles a <sc-helmet> node into an injector function.
symbols.description: Captures helmet children and returns a builder that appends or updates head elements when rendered.

symbols.does: Factory for dynamic pseudo-class styles.
symbols.description: Creates a <style> sheet on demand and inserts rules for style-* pseudo classes, caching generated class names.

symbols.does: Factory for the component registry.
symbols.description: Returns get, bump, and bumpAll functions over an object map of registry entries with html, template, Logic, streaming flags, version, and subscribers.

symbols.does: Retrieves or creates a registry entry.
symbols.description: Returns the existing entry for a name or initializes a default empty entry.

symbols.does: Notifies subscribers that a registry entry changed.
symbols.description: Increments the entry version and calls each subscriber function.

symbols.does: Factory that assembles the full runtime.
symbols.description: Creates registry, pseudo sheet, helmet manager, external modules, component factory, and host object, and exposes update/adopt/registry API functions.

symbols.does: Fetches a sibling .dc.html component.
symbols.description: Loads the named component's .dc.html file relative to COMPONENT_DIR, parses it, and applies template/logic/props when not already fetched.

symbols.does: Updates and compiles a component's template.
symbols.description: Stores the HTML, compiles it via compileTemplate, and bumps the registry entry.

symbols.does: Updates and evaluates a component's logic.
symbols.description: Evaluates the logic source with evalDcLogic, records any error, stores the Logic class, and bumps the entry.

symbols.does: Toggles streaming flags on a registry entry.
symbols.description: Sets htmlStreaming or jsStreaming and toggles the sc-dc-streaming class on the document element if any entry is streaming.

symbols.does: Applies a streamed or final update to a component.
symbols.description: Routes html/js/props updates, marks fetched when streaming, and delegates to updateHtml, updateJs, or parseDataProps.

symbols.does: Sets prop overrides on a registry entry.
symbols.description: Stores a shallow copy of overrides and bumps the entry so instances re-render.

symbols.does: Adopts an already parsed DC document into the registry.
symbols.description: Applies props, preview, template, and logic from a parseDcDocument/Text result.

symbols.does: Hides raw <x-dc> elements via CSS.
symbols.description: Injects a style rule that hides the template source element before rendering.

symbols.does: Injects a script tag and returns a promise.
symbols.description: Creates a script element with src, integrity, and crossOrigin, resolves on load, rejects on error.

symbols.does: Loads React and ReactDOM UMD bundles.
symbols.description: Returns immediately if both are present, otherwise loads both scripts in parallel via loadScript.

symbols.does: Initializes the runtime and exposes the window API.
symbols.description: Creates the runtime, prepends base CSS, defines __dcUpdate/__dcBoot/__dcAnnotatedTemplate/etc on window, and boots when DOM is ready.

symbols.does: Posts the boot message to the parent window.
symbols.description: Sends rootName, propsMeta, and preview via postMessage to window.parent after boot or props update.
